goal: Build a PLC-controlled assembly fixture: Modbus signal triggers fixture clamp/unclamp, Franka inserts part during clamp window. Realizes Phase 9 yrkesroll #13 (industrial integration / PLC engineer persona). Uses M2 modbus_tcp_bridge_attach.
thoughts: Real PLC-in-the-loop pattern: external mock Modbus server controls fixture state via holding register HR0 (0=open, 1=clamped).
Fixture is a virtual 'clamp' — PhysicsFixedJoint applied/removed based on HR0. Robot inserts part during clamp window.
M2 modbus_tcp_bridge polls HR0 at 10Hz. /World/Fixture/clamped attribute updated.
For canonical-build: just verify bridge attach + clamp prim creation. Runtime control is via external mock server (test rig).
Builds on CP-NEW-plc-conveyor (M2 plumbing) but adds part-insertion task.
code:
create_prim(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", prim_type="DomeLight")
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", attr_name="inputs:intensity", value=1000.0)
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Ground", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, -0.5], scale=[20, 20, 1])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Ground", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

set_physics_scene_config(config={"enable_gpu_dynamics": False, "broadphase_type": "MBP"})

create_prim(prim_path="/World/Table", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, 0.375], scale=[1.0, 0.5, 0.375])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Table", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

robot_wizard(
    robot_name="franka_panda",
    dest_path="/World/Franka",
    position=[0, 0, 0.75],
    orientation=[0.7071068, 0, 0, 0.7071068],
)

create_conveyor(
    prim_path="/World/ConveyorBelt",
    position=[0.0, 0.4, 0.78],
    size=[1.5, 0.3, 0.05],
    surface_velocity=[0.15, 0, 0],
)

create_prim(prim_path="/World/Cube_1", prim_type="Cube", position=[-0.5, 0.4, 0.835], size=0.05)
for api in ("PhysicsRigidBodyAPI", "PhysicsCollisionAPI", "PhysicsMassAPI", "PhysxRigidBodyAPI"):
    apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Cube_1", schema_name=api)

create_prim(prim_path="/World/Fixture", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, -0.4, 0.78], scale=[0.20, 0.10, 0.05])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Fixture", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

set_attribute(prim_path="/World/Fixture", attr_name="clamped", value=0)
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/Fixture", attr_name="clamp_force", value=0.0)

add_proximity_sensor(sensor_path="/World/PickSensor", position=[0.4, 0.4, 0.835], size=[0.06, 0.06, 0.06])

modbus_tcp_bridge_attach(
    host="127.0.0.1",
    port=5022,
    register_map={
        "/World/Fixture/clamped": 0,
        "/World/Fixture/clamp_force": 1,
    },
    rate_hz=10.0,
    mode="client",
)

setup_pick_place_controller(
    robot_path="/World/Franka",
    target_source="curobo",
    sensor_path="/World/PickSensor",
    belt_path="/World/ConveyorBelt",
    source_paths=["/World/Cube_1"],
    destination_path="/World/Fixture",
    drop_target=[0, -0.4, 0.83],
    planning_obstacles=["/World/Table", "/World/ConveyorBelt", "/World/Fixture"],
)
extension_notes: Industrial-integration canonical. PLC fixture pattern requires external mock Modbus server (pymodbus.server) with HR0/HR1 prepopulated. Function-gate N/A — tests bridge plumbing + part-insertion sequence.
failure_modes: Modbus mock server on 127.0.0.1:5022 not running — bridge alive=False
Robot inserts part before clamp_force reaches threshold — part may slide off fixture
Bridge polling at 10Hz means up to 100ms latency between PLC clamp and Robot release
